description: ClawHawk v2 — Rewrite Go backend to Python (FastAPI + uv) and migrate frontend from custom CSS to HeroUI v3 (Tailwind CSS v4)

userStories.title: Initialize Python project with uv
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project scaffolded with uv so that dependencies and the CLI entry point are configured.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml exists with: name=clawhawk, dependencies (fastapi>=0.115, uvicorn[standard]>=0.34, websockets>=15.0), dev dependency pytest>=8.0, CLI entry point clawhawk = 'clawhawk.app:main', python >=3.11
uv sync creates lockfile and venv without errors
src/clawhawk/__init__.py and src/clawhawk/__main__.py exist
uv run clawhawk starts without import errors (can exit immediately if no app logic yet)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Pydantic data models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Pydantic models matching the Go structs so that JSONL data can be parsed and serialized to camelCase JSON for the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: src/clawhawk/models.py contains: Session (all 17 fields from Go struct), ProjectGroup, TokenPeriod, TokenStats, DashboardMessage
Internal models for JSONL parsing: Message, MessageContent, MessageUsage
All models use model_config = ConfigDict(alias_generator=to_camel, populate_by_name=True) for camelCase JSON output
DashboardMessage.model_dump_json(by_alias=True) produces camelCase JSON keys matching the current Go output format
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference the Go structs in internal/sessions/sessions.go for the exact field names and types. The Session model needs: sessionId, timestamp, firstUserPrompt, lastUserPrompt, lastAction, model, contextTokens, maxContextTokens, inputTokens, outputTokens, isActive, waitingForInput, gitBranch, usesMemory, client, cwd, projectName.

userStories.title: Port IDE detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IDE lock file detection ported from Go so that sessions show which IDE is being used.
acceptanceCriteria: src/clawhawk/ide.py contains load_ide_map(ide_dir: str) -> dict[str, str]
Reads ~/.claude/ide/*.lock JSON files and returns {folder_path: ide_name} mapping
Malformed lock files are skipped with a warning (not a crash)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference internal/sessions/ide.go for the Go implementation. Smallest module — good starting point for porting.

userStories.title: Port core session parser and helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the JSONL session parser and its helper functions ported from Go so that individual session files can be parsed into Session objects.
acceptanceCriteria: src/clawhawk/sessions.py contains parse_session(fpath: str) -> Session | None that reads JSONL line-by-line and tracks first/last user prompt, last action, tokens, model, active status
Helper functions implemented: extract_user_text(content), extract_action(content), clean_command_text(s), get_model_context_limit(model), decode_project_path(dirname), truncate(s, max_len)
Active status determined by activity within last 5 minutes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference internal/sessions/sessions.go for the Go implementation. This story covers ONLY the single-file parser and helpers. The grouping/caching/loading logic is in US-005.

userStories.title: Port session loading, caching, and grouping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the session loading pipeline ported from Go so that all sessions are loaded, cached, grouped by project, and enriched with IDE/memory info.
acceptanceCriteria: File-level cache using dict[str, CachedSession] with mod-time invalidation (skip re-parsing unchanged files)
load_grouped_sessions(limit: int = 0) -> list[ProjectGroup] globs ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl, skips subagents/, groups by project directory, sorts sessions by timestamp desc
IDE map loaded via load_ide_map and assigned to session client field
Memory file detection: checks for memory/*.md files in project directory, sets usesMemory flag
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (ide.py) and US-004 (parse_session). This wires everything together into the main data pipeline.

userStories.title: Port token stats aggregation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need token usage stats ported from Go so that the header shows today/week/month usage.
acceptanceCriteria: src/clawhawk/stats.py contains load_token_stats() -> TokenStats
File-level cache with mod-time invalidation
parse_file_token_stats(fpath: str) -> dict[str, TokenPeriod] extracts assistant message usage grouped by date (YYYY-MM-DD)
Aggregates into today / this_week (Monday-based) / this_month periods
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference internal/sessions/stats.go for the Go implementation.

userStories.title: Create FastAPI app with static file serving
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run uv run clawhawk and have the dashboard served on a local port.
acceptanceCriteria: src/clawhawk/app.py creates a FastAPI app instance
Static files served from web/dist/ for frontend assets using StaticFiles
SPA fallback: catch-all route returns index.html for non-API/non-asset paths
main() parses --port CLI arg (default 3333) and runs uvicorn.run()
__main__.py calls main() so python -m clawhawk works
uv run clawhawk starts server and serves the frontend on localhost:3333
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create WebSocket endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to receive real-time session updates over WebSocket.
acceptanceCriteria: src/clawhawk/ws.py contains WebSocket endpoint registered at /ws on the FastAPI app
Polling loop with adaptive interval: 10ms (0.01s) when active sessions exist, 2s when all idle
Change detection: compare JSON output bytes, skip send if unchanged
WebSocketDisconnect handled gracefully (no crash/traceback)
Frontend connects and receives session data updates in real time
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (load_grouped_sessions), US-006 (load_token_stats), US-007 (FastAPI app). The endpoint calls both loaders each tick, serializes DashboardMessage, and sends if changed.

userStories.title: Write core tests for parsing and stats
userStories.description: As a developer, I want minimal tests ensuring core parsing and stats functionality works.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_sessions.py tests session parsing with sample JSONL data, text extraction, and model context limit lookup
tests/test_stats.py tests token aggregation and period boundaries (today/week/month)
Tests use tmp_path fixture for file isolation
uv run pytest passes with all tests green
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update build system and remove Go code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Makefile updated for Python and Go code removed after verifying the Python backend works.
acceptanceCriteria: Makefile updated: build runs frontend build (bun) + uv sync, run executes uv run clawhawk, clean removes web/dist, frontend/node_modules, .venv
CLAUDE.md updated with new build/run commands reflecting Python backend
Go files deleted: main.go, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ directory, web/embed.go, web/handler.go
make build && make run works end-to-end: frontend builds, server starts on :3333, WebSocket delivers session data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Only delete Go files AFTER verifying the Python backend works. Run make build && make run and confirm the dashboard loads with live data before deleting.

userStories.title: Install HeroUI and Tailwind CSS v4
userStories.description: As a developer, I need HeroUI v3 and Tailwind CSS v4 installed so that components and utility classes are available.
acceptanceCriteria: @heroui/react, @heroui/styles, tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/vite added to frontend/package.json
Tailwind Vite plugin added to frontend/vite.config.ts plugins array
Dev server starts without errors (cd frontend && bun run dev)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Page may look broken after this step — that's expected since styles haven't been configured yet.

userStories.title: Configure dark theme for HeroUI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dark theme configured so the app has a polished dark appearance using HeroUI's theming system.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/index.html has class='dark' and data-theme='dark' on the <html> tag
Tailwind and HeroUI CSS imports added to the main CSS entry point (@import 'tailwindcss' then @import '@heroui/styles')
Dark theme applied — HeroUI components render with dark styling by default
Page renders with dark background, no white flash on load
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Import order matters: tailwindcss MUST come before @heroui/styles.

userStories.title: Migrate SessionCard to HeroUI
userStories.description: As a user, I want session cards to use polished HeroUI components for a better visual experience.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionCard.tsx uses HeroUI Card component for the card container
Status badges use HeroUI Chip components
Context usage displayed with HeroUI Progress component (or best available meter/gauge alternative)
Tooltips use HeroUI Tooltip for hover info on git branch, memory, etc.
Active/waiting/idle states are visually distinct (different border colors or indicators)
IDE badges with deep links still functional
Git branch and memory indicators display correctly
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Follow HeroUI best practices — don't try to pixel-match the current UI. Use HeroUI's built-in color variants and spacing. Check https://www.heroui.com/docs for current component APIs since some (Meter, Surface) may not exist.

userStories.title: Migrate ProjectBox to HeroUI
userStories.description: As a user, I want project sections to use HeroUI layout components for consistent styling.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectBox.tsx uses HeroUI Card or container component for the project section wrapper
Show more/less uses HeroUI Button component
Responsive grid layout works (1-4 columns depending on viewport width)
Session count and active indicator display correctly in the header area
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: If HeroUI Surface component doesn't exist, use Card or a div with Tailwind classes. Follow HeroUI patterns for layout.

userStories.title: Migrate Header to HeroUI
userStories.description: As a user, I want the header to use HeroUI components for a polished, consistent look.
acceptanceCriteria: Header uses HeroUI Navbar or Card component as container
Filter toggle uses HeroUI Button with visual selected/unselected states
Connection status indicator uses HeroUI Chip (green=connected, red=disconnected)
Token stats (today/week/month) display correctly within the header
Session and project counts display correctly
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: If HeroUI ToggleButton doesn't exist, use a regular Button with className toggling based on isSelected state. HeroUI Navbar component may be a good fit for the header container.

userStories.title: Update App.tsx and remove legacy CSS
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the root App component using Tailwind classes and the old CSS removed so there's no dead code.
acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx uses Tailwind utility classes for layout — no references to custom CSS class names from the old App.css
App.css reduced to only: Tailwind/HeroUI imports, CSS custom properties for arbitrary value references, base body styles, and custom animations if needed
No unused CSS rules remain in App.css
Empty states ('No sessions found', 'Connecting...') styled with Tailwind utilities
TypeScript build passes (bun run build produces output in web/dist/)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The goal is to reduce App.css from ~530 lines to under 50 lines. All component-specific styles should now be Tailwind classes in JSX.

userStories.title: Final end-to-end integration verification
userStories.description: As a user, I want the complete app (Python backend + HeroUI frontend) working end-to-end with all features functional.
acceptanceCriteria: make build succeeds — frontend builds and Python deps sync
make run starts server on :3333 and WebSocket connects
Header displays: title, session/project counts, token usage (today/week/month), filter toggle, connection status
Project boxes display with session counts and active indicators
Session cards display: slug, time ago, last prompt, last action, context meter, git branch, memory badge, IDE badge
Active sessions show green indicator with pulse animation
Show more/less and active-only filter work correctly
Responsive layout works at different viewport widths
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the final smoke test. Run with an active Claude Code session to verify live data flows through.
